query: I have a volume slider (eww scale) and I want the filled part to be purple and the empty track behind it to be dark gray. I styled `scale trough highlight` with the purple and `scale trough` with the gray, but the whole bar comes out gray — the purple fill never shows. What gives?
expected_behavior: Explains scale's GTK sub-elements: trough is the full track, highlight is the filled portion, slider is the knob
Identifies that scale trough matches the highlight too, so when scale trough is defined AFTER scale trough highlight it overrides the highlight's color
Instructs to define scale trough first, then the more specific scale trough highlight after it so the fill color wins
Optionally mentions using min-width/min-height (not width/height) to size the trough
Routes to CSS_REFERENCE.md or SCSS_PATTERNS.md scale sub-element styling
notes: scale trough is an ancestor of highlight, so a later scale trough rule clobbers an earlier scale trough highlight rule. The fix is purely ordering/specificity: trough first, highlight after. A naive answer would add !important or assume the highlight class name is wrong, missing the ordering rule the skill documents.
